skill.name: pymc-bayesian-modeling
skill.description: Build, diagnose, and compare Bayesian models with PyMC using workflows, templates, sampling guidance, and model validation utilities.
skill.summary: PyMC guidance for Bayesian modeling, MCMC diagnostics, posterior checks, and model comparison.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found the command execution, weak crypto, reconnaissance, certificate, and SAM hits are false positives from Markdown backticks and PyMC terminology. No malicious intent, prompt injection, network access, or exfiltration was found. The main security concern is reference guidance that uses pickle loading, plus expected local file writes from examples and utility scripts.

medium_findings.title: Unsafe Pickle Deserialization Guidance
medium_findings.description: The workflow reference demonstrates saving and loading a model with pickle. Loading pickle files from untrusted sources can execute arbitrary code, so users need a clear warning to load only trusted artifacts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The use of pickle.load is direct and security relevant, but it appears in documentation as a local workflow example. No evidence found that the skill fetches or loads untrusted remote pickle files.

low_findings.title: Local File Output Side Effects
low_findings.description: Templates and helper scripts save plots and result files to local paths. This is expected for modeling workflows, but running the examples can overwrite files in the working directory or chosen output path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are explicit and local, using plot or result filenames. The risk is limited because there is no evidence of sensitive file access or network transfer.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives From Markdown and Domain Terms
low_findings.description: The reported external command, weak crypto, reconnaissance, certificate, and SAM patterns were not confirmed. Examples include Markdown inline code and PyMC terms such as sigma, init_dist, pm.AR, Pareto-k, and sample statistics.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review of the flagged locations showed documentation syntax and Bayesian modeling terminology, not executable shell calls or malicious primitives. No evidence found of prompt injection or command invocation.

dangerous_patterns.title: pickle.load Deserialization Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The reference includes pickle.load for restoring a saved model. This pattern is dangerous when applied to untrusted files, even though this skill presents it as a local example.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous API is present at the cited lines. The surrounding context is benign documentation, so this is a warning rather than evidence of malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Bayesian modeling can be hard to structure, diagnose, and compare. This skill gives PyMC workflows, templates, and references for reliable probabilistic modeling.
actual_capabilities: Guides PyMC model setup with priors, likelihoods, named coordinates, and data containers.
Provides linear regression and hierarchical model templates with prior checks, sampling, diagnostics, and predictions.
Explains MCMC, NUTS, ADVI, SVGD, LOO, WAIC, and posterior predictive workflows.
Includes diagnostic helpers for R-hat, ESS, divergences, trace plots, rank plots, and energy plots.
Supports model comparison with ArviZ LOO, WAIC, Pareto-k checks, and model averaging guidance.
limitations: It does not automatically validate that a model matches a specific scientific domain.
Examples require users to supply their own data, variables, and domain-informed priors.
MCMC workflows can be computationally expensive for large models or many folds.
The included pickle example should only be used with trusted local files.

use_cases.title: Build a Bayesian Regression Model
use_cases.description: Use the templates to define priors, fit a PyMC model, inspect diagnostics, and create posterior predictions.

use_cases.title: Analyze Grouped Experimental Data
use_cases.description: Apply hierarchical modeling guidance for grouped observations, partial pooling, and non-centered parameterization.

use_cases.title: Compare Candidate Models
use_cases.description: Use LOO, WAIC, Pareto-k checks, and model averaging guidance to choose between Bayesian model specifications.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Model Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a PyMC model for my dataset. My outcome is [outcome], my predictors are [predictors], and my goal is [goal]. Suggest priors, likelihood, diagnostics, and posterior checks.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt a Regression Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PyMC linear regression workflow to adapt a model for [data description]. Include standardized predictors, named dimensions, prior predictive checks, NUTS sampling settings, and interpretation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Sampling Problems
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these PyMC sampling diagnostics: R-hat values [values], ESS values [values], divergences [count], and trace plot observations [notes]. Explain likely causes and recommend changes.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Advanced Models
prompt_templates.prompt: I have fitted these PyMC models: [model list]. Build a comparison plan using LOO, WAIC, Pareto-k reliability checks, posterior predictive checks, and model averaging considerations.

output_examples.input: Request a PyMC workflow for a continuous outcome with three predictors.
output_examples.output: A structured modeling plan with standardized predictors, weakly informative priors, NUTS sampling settings, diagnostic checks, and posterior predictive validation.

output_examples.input: Ask why a hierarchical PyMC model has divergences and low ESS.
output_examples.output: A diagnostic explanation covering non-centered parameterization, stronger priors, higher target acceptance, longer sampling, and trace plot review.

output_examples.input: Compare two fitted Bayesian models with ArviZ results available.
output_examples.output: A comparison checklist using LOO or WAIC, Pareto-k warnings, uncertainty in differences, posterior predictive fit, and model simplicity.

best_practices: Run prior predictive checks before sampling to catch unrealistic priors early.
Use named dimensions and coordinates so posterior summaries remain interpretable.
Inspect R-hat, ESS, divergences, trace plots, and posterior predictive checks before interpreting results.
anti_patterns: Do not use flat or extremely broad priors without checking generated predictions.
Do not treat LOO or WAIC rankings as decisive when reliability warnings are present.
Do not load pickle model files unless the file source is trusted.

faq.question: Does this skill write code for complete PyMC models?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides templates and workflow guidance that can be adapted to user data and modeling goals.

faq.question: Can it help with hierarchical models?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes grouped-data guidance, partial pooling patterns, and non-centered parameterization advice.

faq.question: Does it diagnose MCMC sampling problems?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers R-hat, effective sample size, divergences, trace plots, rank plots, and energy diagnostics.

faq.question: Can it compare Bayesian models?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports LOO, WAIC, Pareto-k reliability checks, and model averaging guidance through ArviZ workflows.

faq.question: Does it replace statistical expertise?
faq.answer: No. Users still need domain knowledge to choose variables, priors, likelihoods, and acceptable assumptions.

faq.question: Is it safe to use the included pickle example?
faq.answer: Only with trusted local files. Pickle loading can execute code if the file comes from an untrusted source.
